title: Polarographic investigation of the catalytical adsorptive complex behavior of trace iron
author: Lizeng Wang
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Shandong University, Jinan, P.R. China 

  Manuscript received 23 December 1998, accepted 28 December 1998  

 In HAc-NaAc (1 : 1) buffer solution, Fe III -5-Br-PADAP-NO 3  system sensitively presents a reductive wave at a banging mercury drop electrode (HMDE). The reductive wave is a catalytical adsorptive complex wave. In 0.05 mol dm -3  HAc-NaAc and 4.0 x 10 -6  mol dm- 3  5-Br-PADAP solution, the second derivative peak height of the reductive wave is linearly proportional to the concentration of Fe III  in the range from 1.0 x 10 -10  to 1.0 x 10 -7  mol dm -3 . The detection limit is 6.0 x 10 -11  mol dm -3 . The method has been applied to determine trace amount of iron samples of pig meat and tap water.
